assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration that lacks granular maintenance exclusion types
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains 'No channel' as legacy configuration and shows in the feature comparison table that it lacks granular maintenance exclusion capabilities compared to release channels

assertions.expectation: Mentions that 'No channel' does not support 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion scope — only the 30-day 'no upgrades' type, AND per-nodepool maintenance exclusion is also limited
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'only the 30-day "no upgrades" type is available' for No channel and shows in the table that 'No minor or node upgrades' exclusion is not available. Also mentions exclusion translation issues and per-nodepool limitations

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Response clearly shows in the feature comparison table that Extended support (24 months) is available on release channels but not on 'No channel', and mentions this multiple times in the text

assertions.expectation: Notes that rollout sequencing (an advanced feature) is not available on 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Response shows in the feature comparison table that 'Rollout sequencing' is available on release channels but not on 'No channel', and includes a dedicated section on rollout sequencing setup

assertions.expectation: Recommends Regular or Stable channel as the closest migration target (legacy behavior matches Stable for minors, Regular for patches)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Regular channel as the primary target and mentions Extended channel as an alternative, but does not specifically mention Stable channel or explain the behavioral matching between legacy and Stable/Regular channels

assertions.expectation: Mentions Extended channel as a migration path for customers who want maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated section 'Alternative: Extended Channel for Maximum Flexibility' and explains that Extended channel provides 'ultimate control (closer to your current "No channel" experience)' with manual control over minor upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns about exclusion translation: when moving from 'No channel' to release channels, some exclusions don't translate 1:1 and may be ignored — only 'no_upgrades' type translates between the two. Recommends adding a temporary 'no upgrades' exclusion during the transition
assertions.reason: Response includes specific warnings about exclusion translation in the risks section and shows commands for adding temporary 'no upgrades' exclusions before migration in the execution plan

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple gcloud commands for changing release channels, including 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --release-channel regular' and similar commands for extended channel
Response does not reference or mention the specific documentation URL cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/release-channels
